 {"id":"bd-2tq.5","title":"Errno + edge-case conformance expansion for high-impact APIs","description":"Background:
- Error-code and edge-path mismatches are a common compatibility failure class.

Scope:
- Expand deterministic tests for errno correctness, boundary conditions, and corner-case semantics across implemented/raw families.
- Prioritize high-impact APIs from workload traces.

Deliverables:
1) Errno/edge-case fixture packs.
2) Differential expectation tables.
3) Triage templates for mismatch diagnosis.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Declared high-priority APIs have explicit errno/edge coverage.
- Mismatch reports include actionable detail.

Testing/Logging:
- Unit tests for expectation evaluators.
- E2E conformance pack execution in strict/hardened where relevant.
- Logs: trace_id, symbol, case_id, expected_errno, actual_errno, diff_ref.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:05:29.392357602Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T15:05:31.468310814Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["errno","posix","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2tq.5","depends_on_id":"bd-2tq","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:05:29.392357602Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2tq.5","depends_on_id":"bd-2tq.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:05:31.468227207Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2tq.6","title":"Concurrency/signal POSIX suites + differential multi-libc runner","description":"Background:
- Concurrency and signal semantics are critical for POSIX credibility.

Scope:
- Implement dedicated conformance packs for MT-safe behavior, synchronization semantics, and signal interaction requirements.
- Add differential mode comparing glibc/musl/frankenlibc outputs where feasible.

Deliverables:
1) Concurrency/signal conformance suites.
2) Differential runner and report format.
3) POSIX gate integration.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Core MT/signal obligations have deterministic pass/fail evidence.
- Differential report highlights semantic drifts explicitly.

Testing/Logging:
- Unit tests for differential comparator.
- E2E multi-libc conformance runs.
- Logs: trace_id, suite, libc_variant, mode, result, drift_class.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:05:29.524481767Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T15:05:32.540774999Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["posix","pthread","signal"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2tq.6","depends_on_id":"bd-15n.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:05:32.540685872Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2tq.6","depends_on_id":"bd-2tq","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:05:29.524481767Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2tq.6","depends_on_id":"bd-2tq.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:05:31.617882650Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2tt4","title":"Release: process and artifact publishing","description":"Release process and artifact publishing.

Goal: Reproducible release process for FrankenLibC versions.

Release Artifacts:
1. libfrankenlibc_abi.so (Interpose artifact)
2. libfrankenlibc_replace.a (Standalone artifact, L2+)
3. Source tarball
4. Documentation bundle
5. Checksums and signatures

Release Process:
1. Version bump (Cargo.toml)
2. Update CHANGELOG.md
3. Run full CI pipeline
4. Run extended test suite
5. Generate release notes
6. Tag and sign release
7. Build release artifacts
8. Publish to distribution channels

Version Scheme:
- Semantic versioning (MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH)
- Suffix for replacement level: v1.0.0-L0, v1.0.0-L2
- Pre-release: v1.0.0-rc1-L0

Distribution Channels:
- GitHub Releases
- crates.io (component crates)
- Docker Hub (container images)
- Package repositories (future)

Reproducibility:
- Locked dependencies
- Recorded toolchain version
- Build environment documentation
- Bit-for-bit reproducible builds

Success Criteria:
- Automated release workflow
- All artifacts generated and signed
- Distribution to all channels
- Reproducible builds verified","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:04:32.846118966Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T15:04:45.888234519Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["process","release"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2tt4","depends_on_id":"bd-33xi","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:04:45.674532739Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2tt4","depends_on_id":"bd-3f6f","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:04:45.888186539Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2tt4","depends_on_id":"bd-3rw","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:04:45.783302759Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-2uro","title":"EPIC: FrankenLibC v1.0 Completion","description":"Master completion epic for FrankenLibC v1.0.

Goal: Complete FrankenLibC as a production-ready glibc replacement.

This epic tracks overall completion of all workstreams:
1. Symbol Coverage (bd-2vv) - 250→3160 symbols
2. CVE Validation (bd-1m5) - Security proof
3. Fuzzing (bd-1oz) - Edge case discovery
4. Replacement Levels (bd-gtf) - L0→L3 progression
5. Multi-Architecture (bd-1gg) - x86_64 + aarch64
6. Conformance (bd-2tq) - POSIX compliance
7. Documentation (bd-3rw) - Complete docs
8. CI/CD (bd-3f6f) - Automated pipeline
9. Integration (bd-33xi) - Real-world apps
10. Release (bd-2tt4) - Publication

Milestones:
- M1: L0 Complete (current focus)
  - All 250 current symbols stable
  - CVE arena passing
  - Conformance suite complete

- M2: L1 Complete
  - Hardened mode default
  - Real application testing
  - Performance validated

- M3: L2 Complete
  - Standalone static programs
  - CRT bootstrap working
  - No pthread call-through

- M4: L3 Complete (v1.0)
  - Full standalone operation
  - All major applications work
  - Production deployment guide

Success Criteria:
- All child epics complete
- v1.0 release published
- Production deployments +{"id":"bd-2uro","title":"EPIC: FrankenLibC v1.0 Completion","description":"Master completion epic for FrankenLibC v1.0.

Goal: Complete FrankenLibC as a production-ready glibc replacement.

This epic tracks overall completion of all workstreams:
1. Symbol Coverage (bd-2vv) - 250→3160 symbols
2. CVE Validation (bd-1m5) - Security proof
3. Fuzzing (bd-1oz) - Edge case discovery
4. Replacement Levels (bd-gtf) - L0→L3 progression
5. Multi-Architecture (bd-1gg) - x86_64 + aarch64
6. Conformance (bd-2tq) - POSIX compliance
7. Documentation (bd-3rw) - Complete docs
8. CI/CD (bd-3f6f) - Automated pipeline
9. Integration (bd-33xi) - Real-world apps
10. Release (bd-2tt4) - Publication

Milestones:
- M1: L0 Complete (current focus)
  - All 250 current symbols stable
  - CVE arena passing
  - Conformance suite complete

- M2: L1 Complete
  - Hardened mode default
  - Real application testing
  - Performance validated

- M3: L2 Complete
  - Standalone static programs
  - CRT bootstrap working
  - No pthread call-through

- M4: L3 Complete (v1.0)
  - Full standalone operation
  - All major applications work
  - Production deployment guide

Success Criteria:
- All child epics complete
- v1.0 release published
- Production deployments validated","notes":"## Purpose (North Star)
FrankenLibC is a clean-room Rust libc targeting:
- drop-in ABI compatibility with glibc for declared targets (symbols + versions + calling conventions + errno + observable side effects),
- full POSIX coverage plus GNU/glibc extensions where real software depends on them,
- memory safety achieved structurally via a Transparent Safety Membrane (TSM) at the C ABI boundary,
- evidence-driven correctness and performance via deterministic conformance fixtures, E2E scenarios, and regression gates.

This epic is the master closure DAG for reaching a *releaseable* end-state at the currently declared replacement level, and for defining what “finished” means as we graduate to L1/L2/L3.

## Non-Negotiable Contracts (Project Law)
These are not aspirational; they must be enforced by tests/gates:
1. Full POSIX coverage target with GNU compatibility where expected by real software.
2. ABI compatibility is mandatory (symbols, versions, calling conventions, errno behavior).
3. Runtime mode switch is mandatory: `FRANKENLIBC_MODE=strict|hardened` (default `strict`), process-level, immutable after init.
4. Clean-room porting: spec-first; never line-by-line translate legacy glibc.
5. `/dp/asupersync` and `/dp/frankentui` are mandatory build/test tooling (NOT runtime libc deps).
6. Mandatory methods:
   - alien-artifact-coding: formal / lattice / evidence / guarantees; but compiled to simple runtime artifacts.
   - extreme-software-optimization: profile-first; behavior proofs; one lever per change.

## Replacement Levels (What “Finished” Means)
Replacement level definitions and artifacts are part of the release contract:
- L0 (Interpose): `LD_PRELOAD` on host glibc; allowed taxonomy includes `GlibcCallThrough` and `Stub`.
- L1 (Hardened Interpose): L0 but hardened is the default runtime mode; hard-parts and evidence gates become stricter.
- L2 (Partial Replace): no host glibc required for a declared subset of programs/surfaces; CRT/syscall glue and core pthread primitives are required.
- L3 (Full Replace): no host glibc required for general dynamic programs; rtld/loader hard-parts must be real.

Current claim (see README + `tests/conformance/replacement_levels.json`): L0.

## Mode Contract (Strict vs Hardened)
Strict mode:
- objective: maximize ABI-compatible observational equivalence on defined inputs.
- repairs are NOT admissible; invalid/undefined inputs must not be “helpfully rewritten”.

Hardened mode:
- objective: prevent memory-unsafe outcomes via deterministic repair/deny actions.
- every repair/deny path must emit auditable evidence.

Mode selection must be deterministic and immutable for the process lifetime.

## TSM Contract (Transparent Safety Membrane)
The ABI boundary is treated as an untrusted input surface.
Every entrypoint performs mode-aware validation and then routes:
`ABI -> membrane (validate/sanitize/repair/audit) -> core (safe kernels) | raw syscalls | controlled callthrough (L0 only)`.

TSM pipeline is explicitly performance-budgeted:
- strict overhead target: <20ns/call for membrane-gated hot paths
- hardened overhead target: <200ns/call for membrane-gated hot paths

## Evidence-Driven Completion (What Closes Beads)
No feature is “done” without:
- fixture-based conformance evidence (strict + hardened where relevant),
- benchmark evidence (baseline + delta; plus isomorphism/behavior proof for optimizations),
- deterministic E2E coverage for representative real programs (LD_PRELOAD smoke/stress/fault/stability),
- structured logs that are joinable across artifacts (trace_id, mode, gate, symbol/api_family, outcome, timing, artifact refs).

The closure program is centered around:
- `bd-5fw`: release-exit contract + deterministic closure protocol.
- `bd-33p`: evidence/logging unification and compliance gates.
- `bd-b5a`: deterministic E2E suite.
- `bd-15n` + `bd-2tq`: conformance expansion and POSIX suite.
- `bd-30o`: optimization closure with proofs.
- `bd-29b`: runtime config/env truth and drift prevention.

## Phase Map (From PLAN_TO_PORT_GLIBC_TO_RUST.md, but Self-Contained Here)
This is the canonical phase model for the port, expressed as bead workstreams:

Phase 0: Spec Lock + Scope Matrix
- Deliver: explicit scope ledger (what we claim), strict/hardened semantics per family, and traceability anchors.
- Evidence: matrix rows for critique beads; fixtures reference POSIX/C/TSM specs; no hidden exclusions.
- Primary beads: `bd-2vv` (surface classification), `bd-15n` (fixtures), `bd-29b` (runtime knobs), `bd-3rw` (docs drift gates).

Phase 1: ABI Spine + Mode Gate
- Deliver: a stable ABI veneer + immutable mode initialization + per-call context propagation.
- Evidence: unit/e2e tests proving strict/hardened routing and trace propagation.
- Primary beads: `bd-33p` (trace/evidence contract), plus ABI/core/membrane integration beads under symbol families.

Phase 2: Provenance Fabric + Allocator Safety Core
- Deliver: provenance/lifetime metadata, quarantine, and allocator boundary correctness.
- Evidence: strict allocator fixtures + hardened healing oracle + CVE corpus scenarios.
- Primary beads: `bd-1m5` (CVE arena), `bd-1oz` (fuzz), conformance tasks under `bd-15n`.

Phase 3: Bootstrap API Completion (String/Memory)
- Deliver: mem*/str* family correctness and performance with strict/hardened split.
- Evidence: fixtures + benchmarks + hot-kernel behavior proofs.
- Primary beads: conformance/benchmark work under `bd-15n` and `bd-30o`.

Phase 4: Full POSIX Expansion
- Deliver: grow symbol surface while maintaining evidence discipline.
- Evidence: every added family ships fixtures + E2E + perf deltas.
- Primary beads: `bd-2vv` (symbol expansion waves), `bd-2tq` (suite), `bd-b5a` (E2E).

Phase 5: Symbol/Version Fidelity + Loader Reality
- Deliver: version scripts and rtld/CRT/dlfcn surfaces become real as replacement level increases.
- Evidence: ABI/symbol/version diff gates; loader/CRT acceptance fixtures.
- Primary beads: `bd-gtf` (replacement levels), `bd-1j4` (hard parts roadmap), `bd-qwm` (CRT), `bd-h5x` (callthrough removal).

Phase 6: 100% Coverage Closure
- Deliver: close remaining parity gaps for declared POSIX/GNU target set.
- Evidence: final release rehearsal produces complete dossier; no manual judgment.
- Primary beads: `bd-226` (release rehearsal) and all prerequisite epics.

## Proof-Obligation Index (Operationalized as Evidence)
We treat “proof” as a bundle of:
- deterministic fixtures,
- invariant/property tests,
- E2E failure-injection cases,
- and (where relevant) certified artifacts (rewrites/tables) checked by gates.

Release-critical obligations and where they live in the bead graph:
- Strict refinement (defined inputs): `bd-15n` + `bd-2tq` + release-dossier gates in `bd-5fw`.
- Hardened safety (no memory-unsafe outcomes through libc boundary): healing-oracle and adversarial suites in `bd-1m5` + `bd-1oz` + E2E packs in `bd-b5a`.
- Deterministic replay (same inputs/mode -> same decisions/logs): determinism/invariant suite under `bd-25n` and evidence compliance under `bd-33p`.
- Superoptimization soundness (hot kernels): behavior proof ledger and perf gates under `bd-30o`.
- Mode immutability and config truth: env truth pass under `bd-29b`.

## How To Run The Closure Program
Core gate:
- `scripts/ci.sh`

Extended gates (heavier, deterministic, and release-oriented):
- `FRANKENLIBC_EXTENDED_GATES=1 scripts/ci.sh`

Release rehearsal (must become one-command deterministic dossier):
- owned by `bd-226` and contract-defined under `bd-5fw`.

## Multi-Agent Coordination Reality
Active agent identities (from bead assignments):
- BronzeTower, Codex, CrimsonCove, OliveBeacon, OrangeGlacier, RusticCastle, SageCreek, WhiteMeadow, opus-tsm.

Agent Mail status:
- As of 2026-02-12, MCP Agent Mail health is `red` (server overloaded). Coordination is via bead comments, and file-level coordination must be handled explicitly before 12T15:05:08.759652426Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2uro","depends_on_id":"bd-gtf","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:05:08.389981106Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2vb","title":"Stub census: static inventory of todo!/unimplemented!/panic placeholders by exported symbol","description":"Critique mapping: #3.

Deliverables:
- Deterministic static census report with symbol ownership and call-family tags.
- Tag each stub by risk: startup-critical/threading/netdb/locale/iconv/other.

Acceptance:
- Census regenerates identically on repeated runs.
- Report is machine-consumable by prioritization tooling.

Evidence & Test Requirements:
- Add comprehensive unit tests (happy-path, negative, adversarial, and regression cases) for all changed logic.
- Add or update deterministic e2e scripts that exercise strict and hardened modes where relevant.
- Emit structured detailed logs for test/e2e runs (trace_id, mode, API/symbol, outcome, errno, timing, and artifact refs).
- Bead cannot close until test commands and log artifact locations are documented.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CrimsonCove","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:48:09.647069044Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T05:44:59.125392199Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T05:44:59.125310456Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["analysis","critique","stubs"]}
 {"id":"bd-2vf","title":"Kernel Infra: Controller registration + snapshot schema guidelines","description":"Define the standard integration pattern for adding a new runtime_math controller.

Checklist:
- Add module file + pub mod.
- Add controller field to RuntimeMathKernel + cached state atomics.
- Update decide() to apply conservative escalation merges.
- Update observe_validation_result() to feed controller and cache state.
- Add snapshot fields (additive) with documentation.
- Update fusion severity vector and SIGNALS constant.

Goal:
- Future kernels become routine, reviewable changes.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"GentleOwl","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:31:47.874704009Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T17:27:22.900723909Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T17:27:22.900691729Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":25,"issue_id":"bd-2vf","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Claimed by LavenderStone. Starting work on controller registration + snapshot schema guidelines.","created_at":"2026-02-10T17:20:27Z"},{"id":31,"issue_id":"bd-2vf","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Guidelines Complete (GentleOwl)

Document: crates/glibc-rs-membrane/src/runtime_math/controller_registration_guidelines.md

### 8-Step Checklist:

1. **Create module file** — exports: State enum (0..3), Summary struct, Controller struct with new/observe_and_update/state/summary. Required invariants: deterministic init, O(1) updates, saturating arithmetic.
2. **Declare pub mod** — alphabetical order in mod.rs declarations + use imports.
3. **Add Mutex<Controller> field** — to RuntimeMathKernel struct.
4. **Add cached AtomicU8** — cached_<name>_state for hot-path severity reads.
5. **Initialize in new()** — Mutex::new + AtomicU8::new(0).
6. **Wire into observe_validation_result()** — Tier A (cadence-gated meta on base_severity) or Tier B (domain-specific base). CRITICAL: no Mutex in fusion severity literal.
7. **Add to fusion severity vector** — increment META_SEVERITY_LEN, update fusion::SIGNALS, add cached load at next index.
8. **Add snapshot fields** — additive only, document units/range, wire in snapshot() method.

### Key Constraints Documented:
- No Mutex locks in fusion severity aggregation literal (enforced by compile-time test)
- No float math on strict decide() hot path (use integer ppm)
- Snapshot schema is additive-only (never remove/rename)
- Conservative merge: new controllers can only escalate, never de-escalate
- All controllers must impl Default
- State enum values must be contiguous 0..3

### Reference Implementation:
- alpha_investing as the canonical example (468 lines, full integration verified)","created_at":"2026-02-10T17:27:18Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2vv","title":"EPIC: Full glibc Symbol Coverage (250 → 3160)","description":"Goal: Expand symbol coverage from 250 to 3160+ symbols.

Current reality (reality_report.v1.json):
- Total exported: 250 | Implemented: 113 | RawSyscall: 83 | GlibcCallThrough: 54 | Stub: 0

Strategy:
1. Prioritize by real-world call frequency (LD_PRELOAD traces)
2. Work subsystem-by-subsystem (string→stdlib→stdio→math→pthread→socket→time→signal→loader)
3. Each symbol classified with support taxonomy
4. Conformance fixtures for every new symbol
5. Performance validation within budgets (<20ns strict, <200ns hardened)

Success Criteria:
- All 3,160 symbols classified in support_matrix.json
- Zero stubs in top 500 most-called symbols
- E2E smoke passes with real-world programs (bash, python, nginx, postgresql)

Verification Mandate:
- Unit tests (positive/negative/edge)
- Deterministic e2e scripts (strict + hardened)
- Structured logs (trace_id, mode, API, outcome, timing)
- Test commands documented before close","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-12T14:57:39.781120646Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T15:03:23.642856370Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["completion","coverage"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2vv","depends_on_id":"bd-144","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:03:23.642813499Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vv","depends_on_id":"bd-25n","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:03:23.423819838Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vv","depends_on_id":"bd-2ez","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:03:23.533258169Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
@@ -218,7 +218,8 @@
 {"id":"bd-5fw","title":"Completion Program: Final release-exit contract + deterministic closure protocol","description":"Background:
- Current project state has strong partial coverage and many gates, but final completion is still fragmented across open epics and in-progress subsystem tasks.
- We need one deterministic closure contract so future execution and review do not rely on implied assumptions.

Goal:
- Define and enforce the exact end-state required to claim “finished, correct, and optimized” for the current declared replacement level.

Deliverables:
1) Explicit release-exit checklist for L0 (interpose) with objective pass/fail checks.
2) Staged upgrade requirements for L1/L2 progression (what must be true before claim changes).
3) Artifact bundle spec for every release candidate:
   - conformance reports,
   - reality report,
   - replacement/profile reports,
   - perf baselines + deltas,
   - evidence/log indices.
4) Gate ordering and ownership (which scripts/tests run in what order and why).

Acceptance Criteria:
- A clean checkout can run a single scripted release dry-run and produce a complete pass/fail dossier.
- No “manual interpretation” required to determine readiness.
- Any missing artifact or breached threshold fails deterministically with actionable diagnostics.

Test and Logging Requirements:
- Add unit tests for checklist parsing/validation logic.
- Add end-to-end release-dry-run script tests (strict and hardened where relevant).
- Emit structured run logs including trace_id, mode, gate_name, outcome, errno/exit_code, timing, and artifact paths.

Optimization/Proof Requirements (Extreme Optimization skill):
- Include benchmark baseline references for all release-critical hot paths.
- Require isomorphism proof references for any optimization included in the release window.

Porting/Spec-first Requirements (Porting-to-Rust skill):
- Reference behavior contracts from PLAN/EXISTING/PROPOSED/FEATURE_PARITY docs directly in acceptance checks so closure is specification-backed.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-12T14:59:34.154973750Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T15:07:41.795570251Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["critique","release","roadmap","verification"]}
 {"id":"bd-5fw.1","title":"Closure contract schema v1: objective release invariants by replacement level","description":"Background:
- We need an explicit, machine-checkable definition of "finished" for L0/L1/L2/L3 so release claims are objective.

Scope:
- Define a canonical closure contract schema with versioning.
- Encode required invariants per level: symbol support claims, call-through limits, test gates, perf ceilings, artifact proofs.

Deliverables:
1) `closure_contract.v1` schema (human+machine readable).
2) Level-specific obligation matrix with pass/fail predicates.
3) Mapping from each obligation to a concrete command/artifact path.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Contract can be validated automatically.
- No requirement remains ambiguous or manual-only.
- Any unmet invariant yields deterministic failure reason.

Rationale:
- This creates a stable control plane for all downstream beads and prevents claim drift.

Testing/Logging:
- Unit tests for schema validation and invariant rule parsing.
- E2E dry-run over intentionally broken contracts.
- Structured logs: trace_id, level, invariant_id, check_cmd, result, artifact_ref.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"Codex","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:03:12.361423630Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T22:01:46.445449915Z","closed_at":"2026-02-12T22:01:46.445431891Z","close_reason":"Implemented closure_contract.v1 schema + deterministic validator gate + broken-contract e2e tests + structured log enforcement","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["closure","governance","release"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-5fw.1","depends_on_id":"bd-5fw","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:03:12.361423630Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":103,"issue_id":"bd-5fw.1","author":"Codex","text":"Starting bd-5fw.1. Plan: (1) define closure_contract.v1 schema and level obligations in docs, (2) implement machine validator + deterministic diagnostics, (3) add unit tests and broken-contract tests, (4) wire structured release-check logs.","created_at":"2026-02-12T21:53:10Z"},{"id":106,"issue_id":"bd-5fw.1","author":"Codex","text":"Delivered closure contract schema + validator gate + integration tests.\n\nArtifacts:\n- tests/conformance/closure_contract.v1.json\n- scripts/check_closure_contract.sh\n- crates/frankenlibc-harness/tests/closure_contract_test.rs\n- scripts/ci.sh (extended gates now include closure contract gate)\n\nVerification:\n- cargo test -p frankenlibc-harness --test closure_contract_test -- --nocapture (6 passed)\n- scripts/check_closure_contract.sh (PASS)\n\nContract supports machine-validated level obligations (L0-L3), transition invariant mapping, deterministic failure reasons, and structured JSONL logs with required fields (trace_id, level, invariant_id, check_cmd, result, artifact_ref).","created_at":"2026-02-12T22:01:46Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-5fw.2","title":"Deterministic gate orchestration DAG with fail-fast + resume semantics","description":"Background:
- A closure contract is only useful if there is one deterministic execution order for all gates.

Scope:
- Define and implement canonical gate order: lint -> unit -> conformance -> e2e -> perf -> docs/reports -> release dossier.
- Implement fail-fast semantics with reproducible retries and explicit resume points.

Deliverables:
1) Gate DAG with required inputs/outputs per step.
2) Runner orchestration spec and implementation notes.
3) Operator runbook for partial reruns without invalidating evidence.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Same inputs always produce same gate order and same result.
- Resume behavior is deterministic and auditable.

Rationale:
- Prevents ad-hoc gate sequencing and accidental green builds.

Testing/Logging:
- Unit tests for DAG validity and resume logic.
- E2E tests for fail-fast and resume flows.
- Logs: trace_id, gate_name, prereq_hash, status, duration_ms, resume_token.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"Codex","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:03:12.474554818Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T22:05:32.355931474Z","closed_at":"2026-02-12T22:05:32.337574584Z","close_reason":"Implemented release gate DAG artifact + deterministic fail-fast/resume runner + runbook + integration tests","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["orchestration","release","verification"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-5fw.2","depends_on_id":"bd-5fw","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:03:12.474554818Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-5fw.2","depends_on_id":"bd-5fw.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:03:14.671238102Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":107,"issue_id":"bd-5fw.2","author":"Codex","text":"Starting bd-5fw.2 immediately after closing bd-5fw.1. Plan: define deterministic release gate DAG artifact + implement fail-fast/resume orchestrator script + add harness tests for ordering, fail-fast, and resume-token determinism.","created_at":"2026-02-12T22:02:04Z"},{"id":109,"issue_id":"bd-5fw.2","author":"Codex","text":"Delivered deterministic release gate DAG + fail-fast/resume runner + runbook + tests.\n\nArtifacts:\n- tests/conformance/release_gate_dag.v1.json\n- scripts/release_dry_run.sh\n- tests/conformance/release_gate_runner_runbook.md\n- crates/frankenlibc-harness/tests/release_gate_dag_test.rs\n- scripts/ci.sh (extended gates now include release dry-run orchestration)\n\nVerification:\n- cargo test -p frankenlibc-harness --test release_gate_dag_test -- --nocapture (5 passed)\n- scripts/release_dry_run.sh --mode dry-run (PASS)\n\nFail-fast/resume semantics validated with intentional e2e gate failure and deterministic resume token replay (resume_skip for prior gates; restart at failed gate index).","created_at":"2026-02-12T22:05:32Z"}]}
-{"id":"bd-5fw.3","title":"Release artifact dossier spec + integrity validator","description":"Background:
- Final readiness depends on evidence completeness, not just exit codes.

Scope:
- Specify required artifact dossier: conformance outputs, replacement profile, support/reality reports, perf baselines, logs index, decision explainability bundle.
- Build a validator that enforces completeness and integrity (checksums + schema validation).

Deliverables:
1) Artifact dossier manifest format.
2) Integrity validator and missing-artifact diagnostics.
3) Compatibility policy for dossier format evolution.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Missing or malformed artifact blocks release deterministically.
- Dossier can be revalidated independently after generation.

Rationale:
- Ensures future audits can reconstruct exactly why a release was accepted.

Testing/Logging:
- Unit tests for manifest parser and checksum verification.
- E2E tests for success, missing file, and corrupt artifact scenarios.
- Logs: trace_id, artifact_id, schema_version, digest, validation_result.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:03:12.591070967Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T15:06:13.384138217Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["artifacts","diagnostics","release"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-5fw.3","depends_on_id":"bd-15n.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:03:16.448365477Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-5fw.3","depends_on_id":"bd-29b.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:06:13.384091700Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-5fw.3","depends_on_id":"bd-30o.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:03:16.522937289Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-5fw.3","depends_on_id":"bd-33p.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:03:16.238203688Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-5fw.3","depends_on_id":"bd-3ot.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:03:16.632641217Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-5fw.3","depends_on_id":"bd-5fw","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:03:12.591070967Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-5fw.3","depends_on_id":"bd-5fw.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:03:14.770712980Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-5fw.3","depends_on_id":"bd-b5a.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:03:16.338694730Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-5ky","title":"Harness: Regression report for strict vs hardened (latency/risk Pareto)","description":"Create a report that summarizes tradeoffs.

Contents:
- Strict vs hardened deltas in latency (p50/p95) and risk/repair rates.
- Pareto cumulative regret trends.
- Any FDR/alpha-investing and approachability signals.

Goal:
- Make it obvious when a change improves safety but violates latency budget, or vice versa.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"VioletMeadow","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:35:42.328425057Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T18:02:08.192491089Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T18:02:08.192472995Z","close_reason":"Added strict-vs-hardened runtime_math regression report (per-mode subprocess metrics + Markdown/JSON output)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-5ky","depends_on_id":"bd-2ds","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:35:49.994980835Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-5ky","depends_on_id":"bd-3aa","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:35:49.915354009Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-66s","title":"Stub wave 2: getaddrinfo/getnameinfo numeric+files backend","description":"Critique mapping: #3 + #4.

Deliverables:
- Implement numeric host/service resolution and /etc/hosts + /etc/services files backend.
- Deterministic error mapping for unsupported address families/protocols.

Acceptance:
- netdb fixtures pass for numeric and files-backed paths.
- Support matrix marks partial scope explicitly (DNS network backend tracked separately).

Evidence & Test Requirements:
- Add comprehensive unit tests (happy-path, negative, adversarial, and regression cases) for all changed logic.
- Add or update deterministic e2e scripts that exercise strict and hardened modes where relevant.
- Emit structured detailed logs for test/e2e runs (trace_id, mode, API/symbol, outcome, errno, timing, and artifact refs).
- Bead cannot close until test commands and log artifact locations are documented.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CrimsonCove","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:48:09.921326511Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T17:07:39.723409Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T17:07:39.723409Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["critique","resolver","stubs"],"comments":[{"id":82,"issue_id":"bd-66s","author":"CrimsonCove","text":"## Deliverables Complete

### 1. Core resolver module: crates/glibc-rs-core/src/resolv/mod.rs
- AddrInfo struct, EAI_* error codes, AF_* constants
- /etc/hosts parsing: parse_hosts_line, lookup_hosts, reverse_lookup_hosts
- /etc/services parsing: parse_services_line, lookup_service
- getaddrinfo(): numeric IPv4/IPv6 resolution, wildcard fallback
- getnameinfo(): sockaddr_in/sockaddr_in6 to string conversion
- eq_ignore_ascii_case helper
- 35 unit tests covering all functions

### 2. ABI layer: crates/glibc-rs-abi/src/resolv_abi.rs (pre-existing, 519 lines)
- getaddrinfo: numeric IPv4/IPv6 parsing, port parsing, membrane validation
- freeaddrinfo: linked list cleanup
- getnameinfo: numeric address-to-string conversion
- gai_strerror: full EAI_* error mapping

### 3. Status updated: support_matrix.json
- getaddrinfo, getnameinfo, freeaddrinfo, gai_strerror: Stub -> Implemented
- Total: Implemented 87->91, Stub 6->2

### 4. Cascading spec updates
- packaging_spec.json: symbol_distribution, Stub_remaining, blockers
- replacement_levels.json: assessment counts, stub_breakdown
- stub_priority_ranking.json: removed resolv_abi from module/symbol rankings, updated burn_down

### Test commands
```
cargo test -p glibc-rs-core --lib resolv  # 35 pass
cargo test -p glibc-rs-harness            # 213+ pass, 0 failures
```

All quality gates pass: clippy clean, fmt clean, full harness green.","created_at":"2026-02-11T17:07:39.723409Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-66wz","title":"bd-25n subtask: Allocator+membrane invariant/property/adversarial test expansion","description":"Background:
- Allocator/membrane safety claims require broad invariant and adversarial coverage.

Goal:
- Expand allocator/membrane test packs to cover temporal safety, bounds, cache invalidation, and healing behavior comprehensively.

Deliverables:
1) Unit tests for arena generation/quarantine edge cases.
2) Property tests for pointer validation monotonicity and state transitions.
3) Adversarial tests for double-free, foreign-free, UAF, overflow markers.

Acceptance Criteria:
- High-risk allocator/membrane invariants are directly tested and documented.
- Failures report invariant name and violating trace context.

Verification & Logging:
- Unit/property suites with deterministic seeds.
- Structured logs with invariant_id, case_id, mode, outcome, timing.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:02:38.887565117Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T15:02:38.887565117Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["allocator","critique","testing","unit","verification"]}
